skill.name: firebase-ai-logic-basics
skill.description: Official skill for integrating Firebase AI Logic (Gemini API) into web applications. Covers setup, multimodal inference, structured output, and security.
skill.summary: Learn how to add generative AI to your apps with Firebase AI Logic and the Gemini API.
skill.author: firebase

security_audit.summary: This skill contains official Firebase documentation for integrating AI Logic (Gemini API) into mobile and web apps. All 99 static findings are false positives: external_commands are CLI commands shown in documentation code blocks, network URLs link to Firebase docs, and weak cryptography/system reconnaissance flags are false matches on markdown content. The skill is from the official firebase/agent-skills repository with no malicious code, no data exfiltration, and no prompt injection attempts.

content.user_title: Add generative AI to your apps with Firebase AI Logic
content.value_statement: Adding generative AI capabilities to mobile and web apps requires complex SDK setup and API integration. This skill provides step-by-step setup guides, code examples, and best practices for using the Gemini API through Firebase AI Logic on Android, iOS, Flutter, and Web.
actual_capabilities: Guides Firebase AI Logic SDK setup for Android, iOS, Flutter, and Web platforms
Provides code examples for text generation, multimodal input, and chat sessions
Covers structured JSON output and streaming responses with the Gemini API
Explains security essentials like App Check and Remote Config for API protection
Shows how to configure safety settings and function calling in iOS and Android
Includes hybrid on-device AI setup with Gemini Nano for web applications
limitations: Does not cover backend server setup or Firebase Cloud Functions integration
Does not teach prompt engineering techniques or advanced model fine-tuning
Platform-specific code examples cover standard use cases only
Does not address billing, quota management, or pricing optimization

use_cases.title: Set up Firebase AI Logic in a new mobile app
use_cases.description: A mobile developer follows the setup guide to add Firebase AI Logic SDK to their Android or iOS project, configure the Gemini API, and run their first text generation call.
use_cases.target_user: Mobile developer building new AI features

use_cases.title: Add a chat interface with history to a web app
use_cases.description: A web developer uses the chat session code examples to build a conversational UI that maintains multi-turn history using the Gemini API.

use_cases.title: Evaluate Firebase AI Logic for enterprise projects
use_cases.description: A technical lead reviews the supported platforms, API providers (Developer API vs Vertex AI), and security requirements to decide if Firebase AI Logic fits their architecture.

prompt_templates.title: Set up Firebase AI Logic in a web project
prompt_templates.prompt: Guide me through setting up Firebase AI Logic in my web project. I need step-by-step instructions from installing the SDK to making my first API call.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting started with Firebase AI Logic on the web platform

prompt_templates.title: Show me how to generate text with the Gemini API
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to send a text prompt to the Gemini API using Firebase AI Logic and get the response. Include the import statements and initialization code.
prompt_templates.scenario: Learning basic text generation with Gemini models

prompt_templates.title: Implement a chat session with history
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me implement a chat session that maintains conversation history using Firebase AI Logic. Show me how to start a chat, send messages, and retrieve the conversation context.

prompt_templates.title: Use structured output to get JSON responses
prompt_templates.prompt: Show me how to configure the Gemini API to return structured JSON output using Firebase AI Logic. Include how to define a schema and parse the structured response.
prompt_templates.scenario: Getting formatted structured data from AI responses

output_examples.input: Guide me through the initial setup of Firebase AI Logic for a web app.
output_examples.output: First install the Firebase SDK, initialize the app with your Firebase config, then call getAI() to create the AI service instance. Create a model with getGenerativeModel() using 'gemini-flash-latest' and call generateContent() with your prompt. Remember to run 'npx firebase-tools init ailogic' to enable the backend service.

output_examples.input: Show me how to create a chat session that remembers conversation history.
output_examples.output: Use model.startChat() with an initial history array containing role-content pairs. Each message exchange stores user and model roles. Call chat.sendMessage() for new messages. The SDK automatically maintains history so you do not need to manage it yourself.

best_practices: Always run 'npx firebase-tools init ailogic' to provision the backend service before writing client code
Use App Check to protect your AI Logic API quota from unauthorized client access
Start with the Gemini Developer API free tier for prototyping before upgrading to Vertex AI
anti_patterns: Skipping the CLI provisioning step and only running client-side configuration, which causes PERMISSION_DENIED errors
Hardcoding model names in the client instead of using Firebase Remote Config for dynamic updates
Using the Vertex AI Gemini API for simple prototypes when the free Developer API tier is sufficient

faq.question: What is Firebase AI Logic?
faq.answer: Firebase AI Logic is a Firebase product that lets you call Gemini models directly from your mobile and web apps without managing a backend server.

faq.question: Do I need a billing plan to use Firebase AI Logic?
faq.answer: The Gemini Developer API has a free tier for prototyping. The Vertex AI Gemini API requires a Blaze pay-as-you-go plan.

faq.question: Which platforms does Firebase AI Logic support?
faq.answer: Firebase AI Logic supports Android (Kotlin, Java), iOS (Swift), Web (JavaScript), Flutter (Dart), and Unity (C Sharp).

faq.question: Can I use images as input with Firebase AI Logic?
faq.answer: Yes. The Gemini API supports multimodal input including text, images, audio, video, and PDF files. Files over 20 MB should use Cloud Storage URLs.

faq.question: How is Firebase AI Logic different from Vertex AI?
faq.answer: Firebase AI Logic uses client-side SDKs for direct Gemini API access. Vertex AI is a full enterprise AI platform with additional features for production workloads.

faq.question: Do I need App Check for Firebase AI Logic?
faq.answer: Yes. App Check is required to prevent unauthorized clients from using your API quota and accessing your backend resources.
